description_long: This object is a stylized, low-poly flower with a bulbous, purple bloom perched atop a long, slightly curved turquoise stem. The flower head is somewhat spherical with visible polygonal facets and a subtle opening on one side, revealing a reddish-brown interior. The stem emerges from a cluster of elongated, angular green leaves that radiate outward, giving the plant a spiky appearance. The base consists of several thick, root-like extensions in a tan color, spreading outwards as if anchoring the plant to the ground. The overall form is exaggerated and geometric, with sharp edges and flat surfaces, suggesting a digital or artistic interpretation of a wildflower. This flower would stand out in an urban park, garden, or decorative planter due to its unusual coloration and form.
description: A stylized purple flower with a bulbous head, turquoise stem, spiky green leaves, and tan root-like base, featuring angular, geometric shapes.
description_view_0: The flower is seen from the side, showing the purple bloom, turquoise stem, and a spread of green leaves with tan roots visible at the base.
description_view_1: This angle highlights the full roundness of the purple flower head and the outward splay of the green leaves and tan roots.
description_view_2: The flower head is more open, revealing a reddish-brown interior, with the stem and leaves curving to the right.
description_view_3: The opening of the flower head is most visible, showing the interior details, while the leaves and roots are seen from a slightly rear angle.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a radial configuration, forming a roughly spherical, whorled structure with overlapping edges and a visible opening on one side.
